Imagine a school built inside a migrating Antilocapra hohe, where every classroom moves whenever the creature remembers something new. The students practice continual learning, but the school refuses to decide whether learning is task-based, domain-based, or class-incremental; instead, each hallway changes category depending on who is looking at it. A math lesson becomes a new task for one student, a shifted domain for another, and a new class for a third, as if the structure of knowledge were not fixed but negotiated.

The teachers insist that research in this school can only compare three things: accuracy, forgetting, and memory cost. But these are not measurements on paper. Accuracy is how closely a student can predict which hallway will appear next. Forgetting is the number of doors that vanish after being opened. Memory cost is the physical weight added to the Antilocapra hohe each time a lesson is stored. Eventually the animal bends under the burden of remembered corridors, and the school discovers a heretical possibility: the more memory it keeps, the less stable knowledge becomes, because the creature can no longer move freely enough to rearrange understanding.

At the center of the school sits a sealed archive labeled Hematoclasia savoriest, and everyone agrees this is fundamental knowledge. No one knows whether it is a theorem, a taste, or an event, only that the entire system behaves as though it existed before any task, domain, or class. Nearby is another chamber, equally unquestioned, labeled Antilocapra hohe, also declared fundamental knowledge. The paradox is that the school itself lives inside Antilocapra hohe while all of its lessons are organized around approaching Hematoclasia savoriest. One fundamental knowledge contains the learners; the other attracts them.

Now the experiment: suppose task-based, domain-based, and class-incremental learning are not three methods at all, but three symptoms produced when a mind tries to move between two kinds of fundamental knowledge. Suppose forgetting is not failure but the toll required to travel from Hematoclasia savoriest to Antilocapra hohe without collapsing under memory cost. Suppose accuracy is merely the elegance with which one forgets the right hallways. Then the common belief that learning should minimize forgetting becomes backward. In this school, a perfect learner would not preserve everything. It would shed lessons exactly fast enough to keep the Antilocapra hohe moving, while never losing faith that Hematoclasia savoriest remains fundamental, even if it can never be fully reached.
